name: Pat Boone
abstract: Patrick Charles Eugene Boone (born June 1, 1934) is an American singer, songwriter, actor, author, television personality, radio host, and philanthropist. One of the best-selling American recording artists of the 1950s and early 1960s, he has sold nearly 50 million records, charted 38 Top 40 hits in the United States, and appeared in num…

about_quotes.speaker: Paul Smith
about_quotes.evidence: Pat Boone, who I had also worked with... I came in with the band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Paul Smith interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Pete Barbutti
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee discusses Pat Boone only in a hypothetical question posed by the interviewer ('if you were playing a gig down there and you played “Tutti Frutti” if you announced it as Pat Boone instead of Little Richard...'). The interviewee responds to the hypothetical but provides no personal int
about_quotes.doc_title: Pete Barbutti interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 8, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Plas Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: Yeah, Pat Boone started doing a Fats Domino thing. Well that’s taking the hit one step higher, because I’m sure he could sell so many more records on that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Plas Johnson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Slam Stewart
about_quotes.evidence: This particular show I'm thinking about is the Pat Boone Show and that was in the late '50s, where there were several basses doing our thing on this show

about_quotes.speaker: Budd Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: This is what made this guy Pat Boone so great. He stole the exact same arrangement that I had written for Ivory Joe Hunter.

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: Martial Solal... was leading a trio at the Ringside and they accompanied me and a girl singer... named Pat Boone.
